description: Allow recall(query, filters={...}) to narrow candidates by metadata fields before scoring, e.g. user_id, session_id, tags.
competitor_behavior: Mem0's search() accepts filters (e.g. user_id) applied before/alongside semantic search.
vision_fit: Directly improves local-first usability for multi-user/multi-agent apps without adding infra; stays inside existing SQLiteStore.
implementation_notes: Extend MemoryStore.all()/query() to accept a filter predicate; keep default recall() behavior unchanged when no filters passed.
acceptance_criteria: recall(query, filters={'user_id': 'x'}) only returns memories whose metadata matches; existing calls without filters are unaffected.
test_plan: Add unit tests in tests/test_memory.py for filtered and unfiltered recall; verify eval/harness.py score unchanged.

description: Support a namespace or user_id/session_id scoping concept so multiple agents/users can share one store without cross-contamination.
competitor_behavior: Mem0 supports User, Session, and Agent level memory with adaptive personalization.
vision_fit: Needed for any real multi-tenant deployment; must remain optional so single-user zero-config usage is untouched.
implementation_notes: Add optional namespace field on MemoryItem/remember(); recall()/reflect() scoped per namespace by default.
acceptance_criteria: Two namespaces never see each other's memories in recall() or reflect(); default namespace preserves current single-tenant behavior.
test_plan: Add tests covering isolation between namespaces and default backward-compatible behavior.

description: Introduce a lightweight migration mechanism (e.g. user_version pragma + migration functions) so schema can evolve safely.
competitor_behavior: n/a - internal engineering gap identified while planning further storage changes.
vision_fit: Prerequisite for F001/F002/F007; keeps corticore's zero-setup SQLite promise while allowing safe schema evolution.
implementation_notes: Add a migrations module in stores/sqlite_store.py; track schema version; apply migrations on connect.
acceptance_criteria: Existing DBs open without data loss; new DBs start at latest schema version; migration failures raise a clear error.
test_plan: Add tests that open a v1 DB fixture and confirm it upgrades cleanly; add test for fresh DB creation.

description: Benchmark the optional sentence-transformers embedder against the default LocalEmbedder on eval/harness.py and document the tradeoff.
competitor_behavior: Both competitors use real semantic embeddings (dense vectors / LLM-assisted extraction) rather than lexical hashing.
vision_fit: corticore's README already promises corticore[st] closes the paraphrase gap noted in eval/BASELINE.md; this closes that documentation debt with real numbers.
implementation_notes: Run eval/harness.py with SentenceTransformerEmbedder; record recall@k delta and latency/dependency cost in eval/BASELINE.md.
acceptance_criteria: BASELINE.md updated with a second row comparing LocalEmbedder vs SentenceTransformerEmbedder on the same dataset.
test_plan: Run eval/harness.py twice (default and st embedder) and diff results/*.json.

description: Provide async variants (aremember/arecall/areflect) for use inside async agent runtimes without blocking the event loop.
competitor_behavior: LangGraph's BaseStore requires async methods (aget/aput/asearch) as the primary interface, with sync as optional.
vision_fit: Useful for production agents built on async frameworks; must not force async dependency onto the zero-setup sync default.
implementation_notes: Wrap existing sync SQLiteStore calls with asyncio.to_thread initially; revisit true async driver later if needed.
acceptance_criteria: Async methods exist, are covered by tests, and sync API remains the default/documented path.
test_plan: Add async test cases using pytest-asyncio or anyio; confirm no regression in sync test suite.

description: Add mem.export_jsonl(path) and Memory.import_jsonl(path) for backup, migration, and debugging of a store's memories.
competitor_behavior: n/a - common operational need not explicitly advertised by competitors but implied by their dashboards/export tooling.
vision_fit: Supports production operability (backup/restore) while staying dependency-free and file-based, consistent with corticore's local-first design.
implementation_notes: Implement using existing MemoryItem serialization; round-trip through SQLiteStore.
acceptance_criteria: Exporting then importing into a fresh store reproduces identical active memories (ids, text, metadata, status).
test_plan: Add round-trip test: populate store, export, import into new store, assert equality.

description: Tag memories with a type field (semantic, episodic, procedural) so retrieval and consolidation can reason type-aware.
competitor_behavior: Letta/MemGPT distinguishes core, recall, and archival memory tiers with different roles in the agent loop.
vision_fit: Aligns with corticore's explainability goal (why() can cite type), but must avoid becoming a full agent runtime like Letta.
implementation_notes: Add optional memory_type metadata convention; no required behavior change to remember()/recall() by default.
acceptance_criteria: memory_type is queryable via metadata filters (F001); reflect()/recall() behavior is unchanged when memory_type is absent.
test_plan: Add tests tagging memories with each type and filtering recall() by type.

description: Emit structured log records (or a pluggable callback) on remember/recall/reflect events for production monitoring.
competitor_behavior: Zep provides dashboards and debug logs for graph construction and retrieval in production.
vision_fit: Needed for production-grade deployments; should be a thin, optional hook so zero-setup usage is unaffected.
implementation_notes: Add an optional on_event callback in Config or Memory constructor; call it alongside existing TraceEvent appends.
acceptance_criteria: Passing a callback receives every TraceEvent already recorded internally; omitting it changes nothing.
test_plan: Add test asserting callback invocation count matches number of trace events for a scripted sequence.

description: Harden the existing optional PostgresStore (stores/postgres_store.py) with connection pooling, retries, and integration tests against a real Postgres instance.
competitor_behavior: Both Mem0 and LangGraph document production-grade Postgres/Mongo/Redis backed stores as the recommended path beyond prototyping.
vision_fit: corticore already advertises postgres as an optional extra; this closes the gap between 'exists' and 'production-grade'.
implementation_notes: Audit stores/postgres_store.py for pooling/error handling; add docker-based integration tests gated behind an extras marker.
acceptance_criteria: PostgresStore survives a documented integration test suite (create/read/update/delete/concurrent access) run against a real Postgres container.
test_plan: Add tests/test_postgres_store.py integration cases; document how to run them locally (e.g. docker compose).

description: Add a small corticore CLI (list, recall, why, reflect) for inspecting a .db file from the terminal without writing a script.
competitor_behavior: n/a - competitors expose this via web dashboards (Mem0, Zep) rather than a CLI; corticore's local-first file-based model favors a CLI instead.
vision_fit: Fits corticore's zero-setup, inspectable philosophy better than a hosted dashboard; keeps everything local and scriptable.
implementation_notes: Add a console_scripts entry point (e.g. corticore) using argparse, backed by the existing Memory API.
acceptance_criteria: corticore recall <db> <query> and corticore why <db> <id> work against an existing SQLite file and print human-readable output.
test_plan: Add a CLI smoke test invoking the entry point against a fixture DB.

description: Model memories and their relationships as a temporal knowledge graph, similar to Graphiti, instead of flat SQLite rows.
competitor_behavior: Graphiti builds and queries temporal knowledge graphs with entities/relationships and valid_at/invalid_at fact versioning, typically backed by Neo4j.
vision_fit: Conflicts with corticore's core promise of 'no graph database, no server' for the default path; graph backends are tracked as a future optional backend in research/design/DESIGN.md, not a v1 priority.
implementation_notes: If pursued later, implement as an optional MemoryStore/backend (like PostgresStore) rather than replacing SQLiteStore.
acceptance_criteria: N/A - rejected for now; revisit only if a specific paper/ADR in research/papers.yaml justifies the complexity tradeoff.
review_notes: Rejected 2026-07-07: would require a graph database dependency, contradicting the zero-setup default; revisit as an opt-in backend only.

description: Build a full stateful agent execution loop where agents self-edit their own memory blocks and system prompts, similar to Letta Code.
competitor_behavior: Letta Code is a full agent harness where agents rewrite their own context/skills/prompts over long horizons.
vision_fit: Out of scope: corticore is explicitly a memory layer library, not an agent runtime; adopting this would duplicate LangGraph/Letta and dilute corticore's focus.
review_notes: Rejected 2026-07-07: corticore should stay a pluggable memory layer other frameworks (LangGraph, custom agents) depend on, not a competing agent runtime.
